top_categories.workout_context: Race-pace efforts that mimic RBC GranFondo Whistler's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before September.
7,500ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
99ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
RBC GranFondo Whistler's coastal highway with sustained gradual climbing forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 76 miles.
76 miles on coastal highway with sustained gradual climbing burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
At RBC GranFondo Whistler, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of RBC GranFondo Whistler. Train it until it feels boring.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on coastal highway with sustained gradual climbing. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At RBC GranFondo Whistler, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
7,500ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 7,500ft of climbing on coastal highway with sustained gradual climbing does to your power file on race day.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for RBC GranFondo Whistler’s 76 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at RBC GranFondo Whistler. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at RBC GranFondo Whistler requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on coastal highway with sustained gradual climbing to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Race Simulation, VO2max, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 76 miles of coastal highway with sustained gradual climbing in Stanley Park, Vancouver → Whistler Village.
